chemicals.concentration_notes: These highly dangerous alkaloids are distributed throughout the entire plant1. Due to the severity of the effects on the nerves, bone marrow, and cardiovascular system, aggressive supportive and symptomatic veterinary treatment, along with extended monitoring, is essential
chemicals.description: in cats: When ingested, these toxins cause severe systemic and localized damage. They lead to peripheral nerve damage, bone marrow suppression (depression), and cardiovascular damage1. Additionally, they act as severe irritants to the digestive tract, causing pharyngeal (throat) pain, significant abdominal pain, severe diarrhea, and subsequent dehydration1. 4. Notes on
chemicals.name: Vinca alkaloids (which include vincristine and vinblastine)

description: Madagascar Periwinkle (Catharanthus roseus) is a common ornamental flowering plant that is severely toxic to cats. It contains vinca alkaloids — primarily vincristine and vinblastine — the same compounds used as chemotherapy drugs in cancer treatment. Even small amounts can cause serious, life-threatening toxicity in cats.
name: Madagascar Periwinkle
safetyNotes: Toxic compounds: vinca alkaloids — vincristine, vinblastine, vinorelbine; all parts of the plant are toxic
Mechanism: vinca alkaloids inhibit cell division by binding to tubulin, disrupting the mitotic spindle; this damages rapidly dividing cells including bone marrow and GI epithelium
Symptoms: vomiting, diarrhea, severe weakness, low blood pressure, neurological signs (tremors, seizures); bone marrow suppression may develop over days
Despite its cheerful appearance, this plant is as dangerous as chemotherapy agents
Any suspected ingestion is a veterinary emergency

symptoms.name: Pharyngeal (throat) pain, abdominal pain, severe diarrhea, and dehydration
symptoms.notes: These symptoms occur because the Vinca alkaloids (vincristine and vinblastine) found throughout the plant act as severe irritants to the mouth, throat, and digestive tract12. 2. Peripheral Nerve Damage

symptoms.name: Peripheral nerve damage
symptoms.notes: This is a serious systemic reaction to the absorption of the plant's toxic alkaloids, requiring aggressive supportive veterinary care and extended monitoring2. 3. Bone Marrow Suppression

symptoms.name: Bone marrow suppression (depression)
symptoms.notes: The plant's toxins severely depress the bone marrow's ability to function, making immediate and aggressive veterinary treatment essential2. 4. Cardiovascular Damage

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular damage
symptoms.notes: The Vinca alkaloids in the plant are capable of causing profound, systemic damage to the cardiovascular system alongside the nerve and bone marrow effects

treatments.description: The veterinarian will provide intensive, targeted therapies to manage the cat's specific clinical signs as they develop. Based on the symptoms caused by the plant, this involves treating severe dehydration (likely via fluid therapy) and managing significant pharyngeal (throat) and abdominal pain
treatments.name: Aggressive Supportive and Symptomatic Care
treatments.notes: This aggressive intervention is considered absolutely essential because the toxins act as severe gastrointestinal irritants and cause life-threatening systemic damage1. 2. Extended Medical Monitoring

treatments.description: The cat will require prolonged veterinary observation and testing to continuously evaluate its vitals and internal systems over time
treatments.name: Extended Monitoring
treatments.notes: This extended monitoring is critical because the toxins in Madagascar Periwinkle cause complex, deep-tissue complications, including cardiovascular damage, peripheral nerve damage, and bone marrow suppression (depression)
